TITLE: perf: Lazy multi-dim slice loading with LRU cache
LABELS: roadmap, type:perf, size:L, phase:B-robustness
---
## Context

Opening a multi-dimensional TIFF/CZI eagerly converts **every** slice to an RGB888 `QImage` and holds all of them in memory for the session (`docs/11` risk: all slices in memory). A 5D 10×20×3 stack of 2048² frames is 600 slices ≈ 7.5 GB of QImages. Annotation storage is keyed by slice *name* (`stack.tif_T1_Z5_C1`) and is unaffected — only the pixel data needs to become lazy. This issue replaces eager extraction with on-demand extraction plus a bounded LRU cache (current slice ± neighbors), behind an interface that keeps existing consumers working.

**Verify first (mandatory preamble, do before writing product code):**
1. Generate a 5D `TZCYX` TIFF via `tifffile` (the generator pattern from the ImageController test suite), open it in the app, and profile RSS before/after slice creation to confirm the baseline cost and later the win.
2. Confirm per-slice re-derivation: for TIFF, re-opening with `TiffFile` and indexing the array (or `tifffile.memmap`/`aszarr` for page-aligned data) yields the identical slice bytes as today's `create_slices` output for the same `(dimensions, shape)`; for CZI, `CziFile.asarray()` reads the whole array — measure whether per-access re-read is tolerable or whether CZI needs to keep the ndarray (possibly memory-mapped to a temp `.npy`) instead. Record findings in the PR description; if CZI can't be made lazy cheaply, ship TIFF-lazy + CZI-eager and say so.

## Current state

- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/image_controller.py`:
  - `:458-511` — `load_tiff`: `tif.asarray()` (whole file into RAM, `:491/:494`), then `process_multidimensional_image`.
  - `:584-678` — `process_multidimensional_image`: dimension dialog / axes hint, then `create_slices`.
  - `:680-751` — `create_slices`: **the eager site.** Loops `np.ndindex` over all non-H/W axes, per slice `convert_to_8bit_rgb` → `array_to_qimage` (`:717-719`), appends `(slice_name, qimage)`, stores `self.mw.image_slices[base_name] = slices` and `self.mw.slices = slices` (`:732-733`), with a `QProgressDialog` (`:689-728`).
  - Consumers that index by name and only need ONE image at a time: `switch_slice` (`:335-346`, linear scan of `mw.slices`), `activate_slice` (`:779-783`), `switch_image` multi-slice branch (`:389-403`), `add_images_to_list` first-slice w/h probe (`:262-277`).
  - Consumers that need only **names**: `update_slice_list` (`:791-810`), `add_slice_to_list` callers, deletion paths (`:891-894`, `:943-946`), `project_controller.py:453` (save writes slice names + annotations, no pixels), `project_controller.py:279-282`, `image_has_annotations` (`:150-158`).
  - Consumers that genuinely iterate pixels: every exporter (`io/export_formats.py:82, 234, 432, 565, 647, 752, 845, 948` — `for stack_slices in image_slices.values(): for slice_name, qimage in ...`), `training/sam_dataset.py:52`, `controllers/io_controller.py:406-407`, `dialogs/yolo_trainer.py:266`, `controllers/sam_train_controller.py:97/120`, DINO batch `_collect_dino_batch_work_items` (`controllers/dino_controller.py:453-474` — skips stacks whose slices aren't materialized).
- SAM inference guard: `controllers/sam_controller.py` — `_sam_inference_in_flight` set at `:79-81`, cleared at `:151` (ADR-013).

## Proposed design

New `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/core/slice_cache.py`:

- `class SliceProvider`: holds `image_path`, `dimensions`, `shape`, ordered `names` list, and `extract(slice_name) -> QImage` which re-derives one slice (re-open file → reshape → index with the same `full_idx` construction as `create_slices:713-717` → `convert_to_8bit_rgb` → `array_to_qimage`). Name→index mapping precomputed. For CZI, per the verify-first outcome, either the same re-read or a retained (mem-mapped) array.
- `class LazySliceList` (the object stored in `mw.image_slices[base_name]`): wraps a provider + a process-wide LRU (`collections.OrderedDict`, default capacity ~8 QImages, shared across stacks so total memory is bounded). Consumer-compatibility contract, in one table:

| Existing usage pattern | Support via |
|------------------------|-------------|
| `for name, qimage in image_slices[base]` (exporters, trainers, DINO batch) | `__iter__`, one-at-a-time materialization |
| `image_slices[base][0]` first-slice probe (`image_controller.py:266-268`) | `__getitem__` |
| `for name, _ in image_slices.get(base, [])` name-only loops (save/delete) | migrate to `.names` (step 4) |
| `if base in image_slices and image_slices[base]` truthiness (`:263-264`) | `__len__`/`__bool__` |
| `mw.slices is mw.image_slices[base]` aliasing (`:392, 732-733`) | store the same object |
  - `__iter__` yields `(name, qimage)` tuples materializing one at a time (exporters/trainers keep working unchanged — they write each slice to disk and drop it; iteration bypasses/feeds the LRU with `move_to_end` + eviction so a full export doesn't blow memory).
  - `__len__`, `__bool__`, `names` property; `__getitem__(i)` → `(name, qimage)` (used by `image_slices[base][0]` probes at `image_controller.py:266-268`); `get(slice_name)` → QImage or None.
  - `prefetch_around(slice_name)` — pins current ± 1 neighbor into the LRU (Up/Down slice navigation stays instant).
- `create_slices` becomes: build the names list (cheap, no pixel work — progress dialog now only for the dialog-validation phase or dropped), construct the `LazySliceList`, keep `mw.slices = mw.image_slices[base_name]` aliasing intact. `switch_slice`/`activate_slice` replace the linear `(name, qimage)` scan with `mw.slices.get(slice_name)` + `prefetch_around`.
- `image_has_annotations` (`:150-158`) and the save path (`project_controller.py:453`) iterate `(slice_name, _)` — change these **name-only** consumers to use `.names` so saving a project never materializes pixels.
- DINO `_collect_dino_batch_work_items` keeps working via `__iter__` — and its "slices not loaded, open the image first" skip (`dino_controller.py:468-472`) can now be removed for stacks opened in this session, since a `LazySliceList` exists as soon as the stack is added; keep the skip only for stacks with no provider (project loaded, image never opened — same as today).

## Implementation steps

1. Branch `perf/lazy-slice-loading`. Do the verify-first preamble; commit the profiling script under `tests/manual/` or paste results in the PR.
2. Implement `core/slice_cache.py` with unit tests (pure logic + `QImage`, no main window).
3. Refactor `create_slices` to build names + provider; keep the returned/stored object shape-compatible (`(name, qimage)` tuples everywhere it is unpacked).
4. Update name-only consumers to `.names` (`image_controller.py:150-158, 891-894, 943-946`, `project_controller.py:279-282, 453`).
5. Update `switch_slice`, `activate_slice`, `switch_image`, `add_images_to_list` to `get()` + `prefetch_around`.
6. Sweep every `image_slices` consumer listed above and run its tests; exporters and `sam_dataset.build_groups_from_project` should work via `__iter__` without edits — verify, don't assume.
7. Keep `redefine_dimensions` / `remove_image` / `delete_selected_image` dropping the `LazySliceList` (and thus its LRU entries — evict by prefix on delete).
8. Profile again (step 1 file): assert the RSS win; measure slice-switch latency (target: neighbor switch instant, cold switch ≤ the original per-slice extraction time).
9. Tests, docs, senior review, PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Opening the 5D test TIFF no longer materializes all slices: peak RSS bounded by LRU capacity (measured, numbers in PR).
- [ ] Up/Down slice navigation works and neighbor switches show no visible lag (prefetch works).
- [ ] Annotations per slice unchanged: annotate several slices, save, reload, export COCO — identical results to the eager build (byte-compare exported JSON on a fixture project).
- [ ] All exporters, SAM fine-tune dataset build, YOLO trainer, and DINO batch produce identical outputs for a stack (they iterate lazily now).
- [ ] Project save touches no pixels (assert `extract` not called during `save_project` via a counter/spy on the provider).
- [ ] The `[-ndim:]` axis-hint regression test and all ImageController tests stay green.
- [ ] Cancelling the dimension dialog, `redefine_dimensions`, and image deletion leave no stale cache entries.
- [ ] Full suite green: `pytest tests/ -v`.

## Test plan

New `tests/unit/test_slice_cache.py`:
- name→index mapping matches `create_slices`' naming (`{base}_{T1_Z5_C1}` — 1-based, verified against a small 3D and 5D array);
- LRU evicts beyond capacity; `prefetch_around` pins current ± 1; eviction never breaks a subsequent `get` (re-extract);
- `__iter__` over N slices with capacity 2 calls `extract` N times but never holds > capacity images (spy on extract + inspect LRU size);
- extracted pixels equal the eager pipeline's output for the same array (`convert_to_8bit_rgb`/`array_to_qimage` comparison).

Extend the ImageController multi-dim tests (synthetic TIFFs via `tifffile.imwrite` in `tmp_path`): open stack → `switch_slice` to a far slice → annotate → save/reload roundtrip; DINO `_collect_dino_batch_work_items` returns one item per slice for a lazily-loaded stack.

Run: `pytest tests/unit/test_slice_cache.py tests/integration/test_export_formats.py tests/integration/test_sam_finetuning.py tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`

## Docs to update

- `docs/05_building_block_view.md` — add `core/slice_cache.py` to the component table.
- `docs/06_runtime_view.md` — update "Multi-dimensional Image Loading" (names first, pixels on demand).
- `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` — new section: name-only vs. pixel consumers of `image_slices`, LRU sizing.
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — new ADR (next free ADR number): "Lazy slice extraction with bounded LRU"; note the CZI verify-first outcome.
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — resolve the all-slices-in-memory risk.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **SAM in-flight guard (ADR-013):** slice switching during inference is protected by `_sam_inference_in_flight` (`sam_controller.py:79-81/151`); `prefetch_around` must be synchronous, main-thread extraction (no new threads) so it cannot race the SAM worker or evict the QImage SAM is reading — extraction returns a new QImage per call, never mutate a cached one in place.
- **DINO batch expects loadable slices** (`_collect_dino_batch_work_items`, `dino_controller.py:453-474`): iteration must materialize; only keep the skip for never-opened project stacks. The temp-annotation re-sync rule still applies: `image_label.temp_annotations` is a single field re-synced per slice via `_refresh_dino_temp_for_current` — don't touch that path.
- **`is_loading_project`:** project load calls `load_multi_slice_image` (`project_controller.py:206-209`) — building the names list must stay cheap there, and no `auto_save()` may fire during it (CLAUDE.md critical note).
- Slice **naming must stay byte-identical** (`{base}_{dim}{index+1}` joined with `_`, `image_controller.py:721`) — it is the annotation key and the export filename; any drift orphans existing project annotations.
- `mw.slices` and `mw.image_slices[base]` must remain the **same object** (aliasing at `:732-733`); several paths compare/assign them (`:391-392`).
- 16-bit → 8-bit normalization must go through the exact same `image_utils.convert_to_8bit_rgb` path (ADR-010) so lazily-extracted pixels match eager ones.
- Export image-path lookup is exact-key-first, substring-fallback (CLAUDE.md) — do not change exporter lookup logic while sweeping them.

## Dependencies
